expressions.english: a time for meeting someone
expressions.notes:
시간 = time; hour

있다 = to be there; to exist; to have

expressions.english: to have something to attend; to have plans; to have an appointment
expressions.notes: 있다 = to be there; to exist; to have

expressions.english: to keep one's promise
to break one's promise
to cancel an appointment; to cancel plans
Can you promise?
expressions.notes:
약속하다 = to promise, make a promise
-(으)ㄹ 수 있다 = can, to be able to; there is a chance that + S + V

expressions.english: Can you keep your promise?
You are definitely going to keep your promise, right?
expressions.notes: 꼭 = for sure; at any cost; certainly; definitely; make sure to (do something); tight

expressions.english:
Where are you going to meet them?
Where are you supposed to go (to meet someone)?
I'm late for an appointment again.
expressions.notes:
또 = again
늦다 = to be late

expressions.english: I have a lunch date with a friend.
When did I make such a promise?
expressions.notes:
제 = (polite) my; I
그런 = (something) like that; such a noun
약속을 하다 = to promise, make a promise

expressions.korean: 지키지 못 할 약속은 하지 마세요.
expressions.english: Don't make a promise that you can't keep.
I promise you that I won't lie again.
expressions.notes:
다시는 = (not) again
거짓말 = lie
거짓말 하다 = to lie

expressions.korean: 약속이라도 한 못이 모두 다 지각했어요.
expressions.english: As if everyone had planned it together, they were all late.
expressions.notes:
-리도 한 못 = as if + S + V-ed
모두 = all; every; everyone
다 = all; every (thing)
지각하다 = to be late

expressions.korean: 지는 오늘 약속이 있어서 먼저 가 불게 요.
expressions.english: I need to go somewhere today, so I'm going to go now.
expressions.notes:
지 = (polite) I; me
오늘 = today
먼저 = first; before + N
가다 = to go; to leave

expressions.korean: 친구하고 약속했기 때문에 안 가면 안 돼요.
expressions.english:
I promised my friend, so I have to go.
I made a promise to my friend, so I can't skip out.
expressions.notes:
때문에 = because (of), since
-(으)면 안 되다 = shouldn't; must not

expressions.korean: 오늘 저녁에 친구와 약속이 있는 걸 깜빡 잊어버렸어요.
expressions.english: I completely forgot that I was supposed to meet my friend this evening.
expressions.notes:
저녁 = evening; dinner
깜빡 잊어버리다 = to completely forget about something (for a short period of time)

expressions.korean: 이번 일요일에는 다른 약속이 있어서 못 만날 것 같아요.
expressions.english: I have something else to do this Sunday, so I don't think I can meet you.
expressions.notes:
이번 = this; this time
일요일 = Sunday
다르다 = to be different
다른 = the adjective form of 다르다.
만나다 = to meet (up)
-(으)ㄹ 것 같다 = I think + S + will/be going to; it seems like + S + will/be going to

expressions.korean: 오늘은 너무 피곤해서 약속을 취소하고 집에서 쉬었어요.
expressions.english: I was very tired today, so I cancelled my plans and got some rest at home.
expressions.notes:
너무 = too much, excessively; very
피곤하다 = to be tired, exhausted; to be tiring
집 = house; home
쉬다 = to take a break; to get some rest
